title: Navigate To The Primary Operator Station HMI Screens
summary: Use the operator station HMI on the right side of the tipper station to open the documented primary operator screens: Visu_MCP_Dual, Visu_Data, and Visu_Alarm, using the Next Screen drop-down menu, on-screen buttons, or the physical display buttons.
when_to_use: Use this procedure when a station operator needs to access the documented primary operator HMI screens at the tipper station: the main control panel screen (Visu_MCP_Dual), the data screen (Visu_Data), or the alarms screen (Visu_Alarm).
not_for: Do not use this runbook to operate or interpret screens beyond the source-identified operator screens unless supported elsewhere in the source material.
Do not use this runbook for task-specific actions within the opened screens; the source only documents how to navigate to them.

responsibilities: Go to the operator station HMI located on the right side of the tipper station.
Use one of the documented navigation methods to open the required screen.
Verify that the selected function key or selection method opens the expected named screen.

safety_notes: This source describes screen navigation only and does not specify lockout/tagout or production stop requirements.
Use only the documented operator screens and navigation controls identified in the source.
access_or_tools_needed: Physical access to the operator station HMI
Documented function key to screen mapping

access_required: Physical access to the operator station HMI
system_preconditions: Operator is at a tipper station with access to the HMI screen mounted on the right side of the station.

visual_references.description: Operator station display controls showing the HMI navigation buttons, drop-down menu, and physical display buttons used to access screens.
Primary operator control panel screen for Visu_MCP_Dual.
Operator station data screen for Visu_Data.

steps.title: Go to the operator station HMI
steps.purpose: Locate the correct HMI used for operator screen navigation at the tipper station.
steps.instruction: Go to the HMI screen located on the right side of the tipper station.
steps.expected_result: You are positioned at the correct operator station HMI.
steps.healthy_condition: The HMI screen for the tipper station is present on the right side of the station and available for use.
steps.failure_condition: The operator cannot locate the HMI on the right side of the tipper station or the HMI is not available for use.

actions.label: Locate operator station HMI
actions.exact_action: Go to the HMI screen located on the right side of the tipper station.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The operator station display location and right-side controls.

stop_or_escalate_if: The HMI screen cannot be located at the right side of the tipper station.
The HMI is not available for operator use.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: An HMI screen is located on the right side of each tipper station.

steps.title: Choose a documented navigation method
steps.purpose: Use one of the source-approved methods to move between operator station HMI screens.
steps.instruction: Choose one documented navigation method: the Next Screen drop-down menu, the buttons on the right side of the screen, or the physical buttons on the right side of the display.
steps.expected_result: A valid navigation method is selected for opening the required HMI screen.
steps.healthy_condition: You can access screen navigation using the drop-down menu, on-screen buttons, or physical display buttons.
steps.failure_condition: None of the documented navigation methods are available or responsive.

actions.label: Use HMI navigation controls
actions.exact_action: Choose one documented navigation method: the Next Screen drop-down menu, the buttons on the right side of the screen, or the physical buttons on the right side of the display.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The Next Screen drop-down menu, right-side on-screen buttons, and physical buttons on the right side of the display.

stop_or_escalate_if: The documented navigation controls do not respond.
The available controls do not match the documented navigation methods.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Screens can be navigated in three ways: the Next Screen drop-down menu, buttons on the right side of the screen, buttons on the right side of the physical display.

steps.title: Open the main control panel screen
steps.purpose: Access the primary operator screen identified by the source for normal station use.
steps.instruction: To open the main control panel screen, use F3 and verify it navigates to "Visu_MCP_Dual."
steps.expected_result: The HMI opens the Visu_MCP_Dual screen.
steps.healthy_condition: The displayed screen is Visu_MCP_Dual, which the source identifies as the primary screen operators need to use.
steps.failure_condition: Using F3 does not open Visu_MCP_Dual.

actions.label: Open Visu_MCP_Dual
actions.exact_action: Use F3 and verify it navigates to "Visu_MCP_Dual."

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The F3 mapping on the operator station display controls.
The Visu_MCP_Dual control panel screen that should open after using F3.

stop_or_escalate_if: F3 does not open the named screen Visu_MCP_Dual.
The opened screen does not match the documented main control panel screen.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Station operators will only need to use the main control panel screen (F3 button), the data screen (F5 button), and the alarms screen (F6 button).
F5 navigates to the "Visu_Data" screen; F3 navigates to the "Visu_MCP_Dual" screen; F6 navigates to the "Visu_Alarm" screen.

Visu_MCP_Dual is the primary operator station HMI screen for the tipper machine control panel. It is used for tipper movement control and fault reset, and active buttons are shown by green illuminated bars above the buttons.

steps.title: Open the data screen
steps.purpose: Access the documented operator data screen.
steps.instruction: To open the data screen, use F5 and verify it navigates to "Visu_Data."
steps.expected_result: The HMI opens the Visu_Data screen.
steps.failure_condition: Using F5 does not open Visu_Data.

actions.label: Open Visu_Data
actions.exact_action: Use F5 and verify it navigates to "Visu_Data."

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The F5 mapping on the operator station display controls.
The Visu_Data screen layout that should open after using F5.

stop_or_escalate_if: F5 does not open the named screen Visu_Data.
The opened screen does not match the documented data screen.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Station operators will only need to use the main control panel screen (F3 button), the data screen (F5 button), and the alarms screen (F6 button).
F5 navigates to the "Visu_Data" screen; F3 navigates to the "Visu_MCP_Dual" screen; F6 navigates to the "Visu_Alarm" screen.

steps.title: Open the alarms screen
steps.purpose: Access the documented operator alarms screen.
steps.instruction: To open the alarms screen, use F6 and verify it navigates to "Visu_Alarm."
steps.expected_result: The HMI opens the Visu_Alarm screen.
steps.failure_condition: Using F6 does not open Visu_Alarm.

actions.label: Open Visu_Alarm
actions.exact_action: Use F6 and verify it navigates to "Visu_Alarm."

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The F6 mapping on the operator station display controls.

stop_or_escalate_if: F6 does not open the named screen Visu_Alarm.
The opened screen does not match the documented alarms screen.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Station operators will only need to use the main control panel screen (F3 button), the data screen (F5 button), and the alarms screen (F6 button).
F5 navigates to the "Visu_Data" screen; F3 navigates to the "Visu_MCP_Dual" screen; F6 navigates to the "Visu_Alarm" screen.

steps.title: Use physical display selection controls when needed
steps.purpose: Provide the documented method for selecting items when navigating with the physical display controls.
steps.instruction: If using the physical display controls for selection, use the arrow buttons to move to the desired item and press the center button to select it.
steps.expected_result: The desired on-screen item is selected using the physical display controls.
steps.healthy_condition: Arrow buttons move the selection and the center button selects the highlighted item.
steps.failure_condition: The selection does not move as expected or the center button does not select the desired item.

actions.label: Navigate with physical buttons
actions.exact_action: Use the arrow buttons to move to the desired item and press the center button to select it.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The arrow-based selection/navigation buttons and the center select button on the physical display controls.

stop_or_escalate_if: The arrow buttons do not move the selection.
The center button does not select the highlighted item.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Selection/navigation buttons use the arrows to navigate to items on the screen. Press the center button to select the item navigated to on the screen.

steps.title: Use the opened operator screen
steps.purpose: Confirm the operator has reached the needed screen and recognize the primary screen identified by the source.
steps.instruction: Use the opened screen needed for operator work, noting that the source identifies "Visu_MCP_Dual" as the primary screen operators need to use.
steps.expected_result: The operator is on the required documented screen for the intended operator task.
steps.healthy_condition: The needed screen is open, and Visu_MCP_Dual is recognized as the primary operator screen when main control access is required.
steps.failure_condition: The required screen is not open or the operator attempts to rely on undocumented screens for this procedure.

actions.label: Confirm opened screen
actions.exact_action: Confirm that the opened screen is the required documented operator screen and note that "Visu_MCP_Dual" is the primary screen operators need to use.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The primary Visu_MCP_Dual operator control panel screen.
The Visu_Data screen if data access is needed.
The documented screen mappings and navigation controls used to reach the selected screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: The documented navigation controls do not open the named screens.
The operator needs a screen or function not supported by this source material.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Station operators will only need to use the main control panel screen (F3 button), the data screen (F5 button), and the alarms screen (F6 button).

Primary operator-use HMI screens at the operator station are F3 for Visu_MCP_Dual, F5 for Visu_Data, and F6 for Visu_Alarm. The manual specifically notes that the F3 main control panel screen is the primary screen operators need to use.

success_criteria: The operator can reach the documented primary HMI screens used at the station: Visu_MCP_Dual, Visu_Data, and Visu_Alarm.
F3 opens Visu_MCP_Dual, F5 opens Visu_Data, and F6 opens Visu_Alarm as documented.
If physical display controls are used, the arrow buttons move selection and the center button selects the highlighted item.
healthy_conditions: The operator station HMI is present on the right side of the tipper station.
At least one documented navigation method is available: Next Screen drop-down menu, right-side on-screen buttons, or physical display buttons.
The selected navigation control opens the expected named screen.
Visu_MCP_Dual is available as the primary operator screen.
failure_conditions: The operator station HMI cannot be located or accessed.
The documented navigation controls do not respond or do not open the named screens.
F3 does not open Visu_MCP_Dual, F5 does not open Visu_Data, or F6 does not open Visu_Alarm.
Physical arrow or center select buttons do not work as documented.
The operator requires screens or functions not supported by this source material.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the documented navigation controls do not open the named screens.
Escalate if the HMI or its physical display controls are unavailable or unresponsive.
Do not assume use of other screens beyond the source-identified operator screens unless supported elsewhere in source material.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Operator station display controls, function key mappings, drop-down menu, and physical selection buttons.
Primary operator control panel screen for Visu_MCP_Dual.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: An HMI screen is located on the right side of each tipper station.
Screens can be navigated in three ways: the Next Screen drop-down menu, buttons on the right side of the screen, buttons on the right side of the physical display.
Station operators will only need to use the main control panel screen (F3 button), the data screen (F5 button), and the alarms screen (F6 button).
F5 navigates to the "Visu_Data" screen; F3 navigates to the "Visu_MCP_Dual" screen; F6 navigates to the "Visu_Alarm" screen.
Selection/navigation buttons use the arrows to navigate to items on the screen. Press the center button to select the item navigated to on the screen.

missing_details: The source does not provide an estimated completion time.
The source does not specify whether production must be stopped before performing this navigation.
The source does not specify lockout/tagout requirements for this navigation activity.
The source does not provide detailed operating instructions for tasks performed within Visu_MCP_Dual, Visu_Data, or Visu_Alarm.
No dedicated artifact for the Visu_Alarm screen was provided in this packet.
